name: Sentinel 3
area: Global coverage (focus on land and ocean monitoring)
quality: High-quality data from advanced remote sensing instruments (SLSTR & OLCI)
availability: Free and open access via Copernicus Dataspace Ecosystem
update_frequency: Daily (with near real-time data for specific products)
legal_restrictions: Open data policy under the Copernicus program
description: Sentinel-3 provides data for monitoring sea and land surface temperature, ocean color, and vegetation, as well as for supporting environmental and climate monitoring. The mission includes instruments like the SLTR (Sea and Land Surface Temperature Radiometer) and OLCI (Ocean and Land Colour Instrument).
variable_s:
SLSTR Bands (Thermal Infrared & Visible):
  - Bands 1-3: Visible (0.55–1.60 µm)
  - Bands 4-9: Thermal Infrared (3.75–12.00 µm)
 OLCI Bands (Ocean and Land Color):
  - Bands 1-3: UV and Blue (400–500 nm)
  - Bands 4-10: Green, Yellow, Red, and Red Edge (510–780 nm)
  - Bands 11-16: Near Infrared and Shortwave Infrared (865–1650 nm)
spatial_coverage: Global (including oceans, land, and coastal areas)
spatial_resolution: SLSTR: 500 m (for SST and LST); OLCI: 300 m (for ocean color products)
temporal_resolution: Daily (with repeat orbiting, high revisit frequency)
api_protocol:
The Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem catalogue is a web service that can be queried through 4 REST protocols
  - OData
  - STAC
  - OpenSearch
  - Sentinel Hub Catalog API
limitations: Temporal gaps during specific passes; large data volumes requiring significant storage and processing power
